     The Numbers Business Report

MA (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $45,896,028Details
International Box Office $15,324,756
Worldwide Box Office $61,220,784
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,973,823
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,345,744
Total $3,319,567
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Sue Ann is a loner who keeps to herself in her quiet Ohio town. One day, she is asked by Maggie, a new teenager in town, to buy some booze for her and her friends, and Sue Ann sees the chance to make some unsuspecting, if younger, friends of her own. She offers the kids the chance to avoid drinking and driving by hanging out in the basement of her home. But there are some house rules: One of the kids has to stay sober. Don’t curse. Never go upstairs. And call her “Ma.” But as Ma’s hospitality starts to curdle into obsession, what began as a teenage dream turns into a terrorizing nightmare, and Ma’s place goes from the best place in town to the worst place on earth.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$18,099,805 (39.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:75.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$5,000,000 (worldwide box office is 12.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,808 opening theaters/2,816 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $59,925,377

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 31st, 2019 (Wide) by Universal
May 14th, 2025 (Special Engagement) by Universal
International Releases: May 29th, 2019 (Wide) (Egypt)
May 30th, 2019 (Wide) (Croatia)
May 30th, 2019 (Wide) (Middle East Region)
May 30th, 2019 (Wide) (Paraguay)
May 30th, 2019 (Wide) (Lebanon)
Video Release: August 20th, 2019 by Universal Home Entertainment
October 14th, 2019 by Universal Pictures UK, released as Ma
MPA Rating: R for violent/disturbing material, language throughout, sexual content, and for teen drug and alcohol use.
(Rating bulletin 2569 (Cert #51640), 3/13/2019)
Running Time: 99 minutes
Geographical setting: Ohio
Plot point: Kidnap, Social Outcast
Subgenre: Psychological Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Blumhouse, Wyolah Films, Universal Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Sue Ann is a loner who keeps to herself in her quiet Ohio town. One day, she is asked by Maggie, a new teenager in town, to buy some booze for her and her friends, and Sue Ann sees the chance to make some unsuspecting, if younger, friends of her own. She offers the kids the chance to avoid drinking and driving by hanging out in the basement of her home. But there are some house rules: One of the kids has to stay sober. Don’t curse. Never go upstairs. And call her “Ma.” But as Ma’s hospitality starts to curdle into obsession, what began as a teenage dream turns into a terrorizing nightmare, and Ma’s place goes from the best place in town to the worst place on earth.
